commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=69ca69ebd46f701f46d45a2dcc6f80ef2df914c7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a lightweight legacy-state detector so `openclaw gateway run` can skip the doctor state-migration preflight when no known legacy inputs are present, with expanded config-guard tests.

PR surface: Source +355, Tests +428. Total +783 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: a Gateway run with only MSTeams `*.learnings.json` legacy files can reach the new skip gate without any detector returning true, while current main's doctor contract detects those files.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can add the missing shipped MSTeams feedback-learning legacy-source detection and regression coverage on the PR branch.

target.title: improve: skip Gateway migration preflight without legacy state
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`openclaw gateway run` paid the full doctor state-migration preflight on startup even when there was no legacy state to migrate. In the generic Gateway startup benchmark this kept the current-main hot path dominated by legacy state migration/import work and jiti/module-loading overhead.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change gates the Gateway foreground startup doctor migration preflight behind lightweight legacy-input detection, matching the existing read-only command behavior while preserving automatic migration for known legacy sources.

The detector covers:

- core state sidecars and pending SQLite archives
- bundled channel legacy state
- delivery queue, debug-proxy, config-health/update/current-conversation state
- cron legacy JSON/state/run-log stores, including configured `cron.store`
- shipped plugin doctor migration sources (Active Memory, Phone Control, ACPX, Device Pair, Matrix, Nostr, MSTeams, Voice Call, Memory Core, Memory Wiki, Workboard, Telegram)
- configured session stores and config-derived plugin paths
- conservative fallback when the shared-state SQLite probe cannot run

It also avoids persistent false positives from already-archived `*.migrated` plugin sources and normal current plugin-state rows.

## User Impact

Generic Gateway cold startup is substantially faster when no legacy migration input exists. Users with legacy state still get the same automatic startup migration path as before.

Benchmark artifact:

- `.artifacts/gateway-startup-upstream/20260624T001328Z-gateway-preflight-final-candidate/before-after-summary.md`

P50 Gateway-ready deltas vs `origin/main` baseline:

| case | baseline p50 ready ms | candidate p50 ready ms | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| default | 3380.7 | 1882.3 | -1498.4ms (-44.3%) |
| skipChannels | 3087.7 | 1393.6 | -1694.1ms (-54.9%) |
| oneInternalHook | 3060.8 | 1395.7 | -1665.1ms (-54.4%) |
| allInternalHooks | 3056.5 | 1391.3 | -1665.2ms (-54.5%) |
| fiftyPlugins | 3291.8 | 1587.5 | -1704.3ms (-51.8%) |
| fiftyStartupLazyPlugins | 3170.3 | 1391.3 | -1779.0ms (-56.1%) |

CPU profile evidence after the change:

- `.artifacts/gateway-startup-upstream/20260624T001534Z-gateway-preflight-final-cpu/cpu/cpu-self-summary.md`
- skipChannels one-run: Gateway-ready 1415.8ms, CPU 2470ms
- top-profile keyword self-time: `jiti=0.0ms`, `state-migrations=0.0ms`; remaining top self-time is mostly Node package-json reads / process spawn.

## Evidence

Passed:

- `pnpm test src/cli/argv.test.ts src/cli/program/config-guard.test.ts`
  - 131 argv tests + 65 config-guard tests passed
- `pnpm build:docker`
- `pnpm check`
- `codex review --base origin/main`
  - no blocking regression identified after reviewer-driven fixes
- `pnpm test:startup:gateway --runs 5 --warmup 1 --output .artifacts/gateway-startup-upstream/20260624T001328Z-gateway-preflight-final-candidate/gateway-startup.json`
- `pnpm test:startup:gateway --case skipChannels --runs 1 --warmup 0 --cpu-prof-dir .artifacts/gateway-startup-upstream/20260624T001534Z-gateway-preflight-final-cpu/cpu --output .artifacts/gateway-startup-upstream/20260624T001534Z-gateway-preflight-final-cpu/gateway-startup.json`

Validation caveat:

- Full `pnpm test` was run and failed in unrelated shards outside this patch's two changed files. Failures included provider catalog expectations, timing/process tests, backup late-SQLite tests, security scan allowlist tests, and script resolver tests. The failure log is `.artifacts/gateway-startup-upstream/pnpm-test-gateway-preflight-final.log`.
